PozzoDyne™: Engineered to Perform

AggrePlex transforms landfill-destined glass into activated ground-glass pozzolan (AGGP), a high-performance supplementary cementitious material that enhances concrete durability, long-term strength, and resilience while significantly reducing its carbon footprint.

Flagship Product

PozzoDyne™ — engineered to perform.

AggrePlex's flagship Activated Ground Glass Pozzolan (AGGP) — a next-generation supplementary cementitious material engineered to lower carbon, improve concrete performance, and create value from underutilized glass streams.

Lower Carbon ConcreteStronger & DurableCircular SolutionBuilt for Scale
What is PozzoDyne™?

Ultra-fine. Engineered. Repeatable.

PozzoDyne™ is an ultra-fine ground glass pozzolan manufactured from carefully processed glass feedstocks. Through AggrePlex's proprietary material processing, the glass is cleaned, micronized, and classified into a precise particle-size distribution that delivers high performance in cementitious systems.

As a supplementary cementitious material, PozzoDyne™ can replace 20–30% of ordinary Portland cement in the concrete matrix — reducing embodied carbon while maintaining or improving concrete performance.

PozzoDyne™ is designed to support:
Lower-carbon concrete
Enhanced long-term strength development
Improved durability
Increased sulfate resistivity
Reduced chloride permeability
Circular use of glass byproducts
ASR mitigation
Product Specifications

Two engineered distributions.

Produced in two particle-size distributions to support different performance requirements and mix designs.

PozzoDyne™ 20µm

D97≤ 20 microns
D505 microns

Optimized for balanced performance, workability, and general cement replacement.

PozzoDyne™ 10µm

D97≤ 10 microns
D503 microns

Designed for higher reactivity, increased surface area, and enhanced early and long-term performance.

Both products are manufactured using precise micronization and classification to ensure consistency, reactivity, and repeatability across applications.

What are pozzolans?

The chemistry behind better concrete

Pozzolans are amorphous siliceous materials that react with calcium hydroxide in the presence of water to form additional cementitious compounds — densifying the cement matrix and improving strength, durability, and chemical resistance over time. PozzoDyne™ is a new generation of engineered pozzolanic powder made from glass and optimized through advanced particle engineering.

Why PozzoDyne™ matters

Concrete's carbon problem, addressed

Cement production accounts for roughly 8% of global emissions. PozzoDyne™ replaces a portion of Portland cement with a lower-carbon alternative that integrates into existing concrete and cement production systems — reducing carbon intensity while improving material circularity.

01 · Performance

Superior performance

Improves long-term strength, durability, and resistance to chemical attack across demanding applications.

02 · Economics

Cost-competitive

Priced to compete with cement while improving performance and lowering total lifecycle cost.

03 · Supply

Reliable & local

Consistent quality and optimized reactivity, proudly produced in the U.S. for a resilient supply chain.

04 · Sustainability

Sustainable solution

Diverts glass from landfill and cuts the embodied carbon of every structure it goes into.

Competitive Advantage

Better concrete, from the inside out.

Engineered reactivity. Measurable results. How PozzoDyne™ concrete outperforms conventional mixes.

01

20–30% cement replacement

Comparable or improved compressive strength with standard mix design practices — no production redesign.

02

Lower embodied carbon

Direct CO₂ reduction per cubic yard; supports ESG, regulatory, and project-specific carbon targets.

03

Reduced permeability

Ultra-fine particles refine the pore structure — lower chloride ingress and improved durability in aggressive environments.

04

ASR & sulfate resistance

Consumes free lime into additional C-S-H, limiting expansive alkali-silica and sulfate reactions.

05

Strength at every age

Slag-like early performance, ~100% of control at 28 days, and continued densification beyond 56 days.

06

Thermal performance

A denser matrix limits heat transfer — higher R-value, lower U-value, better energy efficiency.

07

Fire & heat stability

Less free lime and more stable C-S-H phases reduce high-temperature degradation and spalling risk.

08

Acoustic & consistency

Higher sound attenuation (STC), plus engineered repeatability that eliminates the variability of traditional SCMs.

This is not just a cement replacement — it's a performance upgrade to conventional concrete.
Sustainability

Lower carbon. Smarter materials. Scalable impact.

LCA Key Finding
Up to 94%

reduction in CO₂ emissions during production compared to ordinary Portland cement (OPC) — validated by Life Cycle Assessment.

CO₂ reduction through cement replacement

Cement is the most carbon-intensive component of concrete. Replacing 20–30% of it with PozzoDyne™ directly lowers embodied carbon per cubic yard — without changing how concrete is produced.

Data-backed decarbonization

LCA-validated performance supports ESG and sustainability reporting, aligns with emerging low-carbon material standards, and gives project owners quantifiable impact.

Circular material strategy

Landfill-bound glass becomes a high-value cementitious input — upgrading existing material streams, extending lifecycles, and reducing dependency on newly produced materials.

Certifications & Standards

Built to be specified.

PozzoDyne™ is engineered to meet established industry standards and integrate seamlessly into existing concrete specifications.

ASTM C1866 Compliance

Standard Specification for Ground-Glass Pozzolan for Use in Concrete

Loss on Ignition (LOI) ≤ 0.5% — consistently below threshold
Fineness — engineered particle-size distributions well within specification
Chemical composition — high amorphous silica content suitable for pozzolanic reaction
Performance criteria — meets or exceeds required strength activity and durability benchmarks

Specification-ready for commercial and infrastructure projects — can be specified as a ground-glass pozzolan per ASTM C1866 within performance-based specs and 20–30% replacement strategies.

Performance-Based Validation

Evaluated through a comprehensive testing framework aligned with industry standards

Reactivity & strength — ASTM C311 (Strength Activity Index), C1897 (Isothermal Calorimetry)
Durability — ASTM C1260 / C1567 (ASR mitigation), C1012 (sulfate resistance), C1202 (chloride penetration)
Physical properties — ASTM C204 (Blaine fineness), full PSD (D10–D97), specific gravity, density, moisture

Documentation available: Technical Data Sheet, ASTM C1866 compliance statement, test data summary, and environmental product profile.

The AggrePlex™ Process

It's not ground. It's micronized.

Powered by Microtec's advanced milling and air-classification systems, we produce ultra-fine powders with tightly controlled particle-size distributions that conventional grinding simply can't reach.

Step 01 · Material Separation

Mixed glass in. Clean cullet out.

The recycled stream rides the screening line: fines and dirt shake through the vibrating deck, oversize non-glass is air-ejected into the reject bin, and only sorted glass cullet makes it to the end of the line.

Mixed Recycled Glass
Post-consumer stream
Vibrating Screen
Fines & dirt shaken out
Oversize Reject
Plastics · Non-glass removed
Sorted Glass Cullet
Screened & sorted
Move cursor to explore
Step 02 · Cleaning & Conditioning

Washed, dried, ready to mill.

Precision cleaning removes adhesives, sugars, and organic residues; drying and conditioning deliver a consistent, engineered glass feedstock.

Sorted Glass Cullet
From separation
Precision Cleaning
Adhesives · Sugars · Organics
Drying & Conditioning
Moisture removed
Engineered Glass Feedstock
Ready for micronization
Move cursor to explore
Step 03 · The Signature Process

Cullet in. PozzoDyne™ out.

Recovered glass enters the UTM Turbo Mill, where shear, attrition, and impact forces explode particles down to the micron level — engineered fineness, not just size reduction.

Recovered Glass Cullet
Post-consumer feedstock
UTM Turbo Mill
Shear · Attrition · Impact
PozzoDyne™
Micronized pozzolan

How we do it

Advanced materials, meet practical solutions.

Ultra-fine production

Engineered down to the micron

Particle sizes reduced to the micron level increase surface area and lift material performance where it counts.

Precision classification

Consistent, repeatable output

Tightly controlled particle-size distributions deliver consistency and repeatability across every application.

Engineered reactivity

Stronger pozzolanic reactions

Optimized particle design supports stronger reactions and improved cementitious performance.

Commercial scale

Built for volume, not the lab

Designed for high-volume production — real supply for real infrastructure, not laboratory-scale results.

Built for scale

Performance that survives contact with reality.

Many advanced materials shine in controlled environments, then falter when scaled. Ours are designed for scale from the start.

By pairing an abundantly available feedstock with proven manufacturing technology, we deliver a reliable supply of high-performance material for construction and industry worldwide.

Micronized glass · PozzoDyne™
Sustainability, simplified

A greener build without the trade-off.

By converting underutilized glass into high-value SCMs, we cut reliance on carbon-intensive raw materials and extend the life of resources already in circulation.

PozzoDyne™ draws on a readily available feedstock — enabling long-term scalability and genuine material security.

Diverted from landfill · Re-engineered
Engineered for scale

Process precision

Engineered output, measured.

D97

≤ 10–20 µm

Two engineered particle-size distributions for different performance targets.

D50

3–5 µm

Median particle size at the micron level — surface area that drives reactivity.

LOI

≤ 0.5%

Loss on ignition consistently below the ASTM C1866 threshold — clean feedstock, low variability.

CO₂

Up to 94% lower

Production emissions vs ordinary Portland cement, validated by Life Cycle Assessment.
